finding: After submitting the application the only confirmation is one tiny line of green text next to the button — no full-screen 'you're in!' moment, no redirect, the form doesn't clear. It looks like nothing happened.

evidence.quote: small green text 'Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.' beside the SUBMIT APPLICATION button
Automation itself missed the success state and re-submitted 5 more times — evidence of how subtle the confirmation is.

persona_rationale: When I send a Snap or post a TikTok I get a big obvious 'Sent!' or a checkmark animation. A thin grey-green sentence I have to hunt for next to the button reads as 'error/did it even work?' — I'd tap submit again and again or just close the tab thinking it's broken.

suggested_fix.current: Inline green line 'Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.' next to the submit button, form stays filled
suggested_fix.proposed: Redirect to the existing /thank-you-for-applying page (or swap the form for a full-width success card with a big check icon and 'You're in — Mike's team will reach out')

finding: The apply form is long for a first touch — 9 fields including 4 'Select' dropdowns (State, Business stage, Budget, Launch timeline), three of them required, before anyone has even talked to me.

evidence.quote: Name* / Email* / Phone / City / State* / Business stage* / Available startup budget* / Launch timeline* / What are you trying to build?
four side-by-side 'Select' dropdowns plus name/email/phone/city and a large textarea

persona_rationale: Apps I use never ask me to fill out a wall of dropdowns to start something — TikTok signup is basically a phone number and a tap. Being asked my 'available startup budget' and 'launch timeline' on the very first screen feels like a job application, not 'step inside.' I'd bounce.

suggested_fix.current: Single 9-field form with 4 required dropdowns on first contact
suggested_fix.proposed: Ask only Name + Email + a one-tap 'where are you at?' on step 1, then reveal budget/timeline on a step 2 — or clearly mark the dropdowns as optional

finding: The big hero stats all show zeros: '0+ Entrepreneurs launched', '$0M+ Snack/Drink sales', '0+ Vending locations'. The proof numbers are literally zero.

evidence.quote:
0+
ENTREPRENEURS LAUNCHED
$0M+
SNACK/DRINK SALES IN VENDINGPRENEUR MACHINES
0+
VENDING LOCATIONS W/OUR GUIDANCE

persona_rationale: A '0' follower/like count is the universal teen signal for 'this is dead / fake / nobody is here.' Seeing '0+ entrepreneurs launched' on the homepage instantly makes me think this is a scam or unfinished, even though there are real reviews lower down.

suggested_fix.proposed: Populate real numbers (e.g. '500+', '$5M+', '1,200+'); if data isn't ready, hide the stat block instead of showing zeros

finding: On the blog/news page the article cards render as empty white outlines above the fold, and one card shows broken-image alt text instead of a picture — the page looks unfinished/broken on first paint.

evidence.quote: blank card outlines and visible alt text 'Top 10 Profitable Products to Stock in Your Vending Machine (Snacks, Healthy Items, Niche Products)' where an image should be

persona_rationale: A broken thumbnail with the file caption showing through is the exact thing that makes a site feel sketchy or abandoned — like an old expired link. My feed apps never show me a busted image; if I saw this I'd assume the whole site is broken and leave.

suggested_fix.current: Blog cards show blank outlines on first paint and a failing /_next/image (500) renders alt text
suggested_fix.proposed: Fix the failing image source/loader, add a fallback placeholder image, and render card content immediately instead of waiting for scroll-reveal

finding: The same section is called 'NEWS' in the menu but the page title is 'BLOG' — two different names for one thing.
persona_rationale: I tap 'News' expecting news and land on a giant 'BLOG' header — small thing, but it makes me second-guess whether I clicked the right thing. Apps I use keep the label the same from tab to screen.

suggested_fix.current: Nav label 'NEWS' leads to a page titled 'BLOG'
suggested_fix.proposed: Pick one name — rename the page heading to 'News' (or the nav item to 'Blog') so they match

finding: Hero copy and body are corporate/buzzwordy — 'launch and scale a profitable vending machine business with minimal time investment', 'Automation & scaling strategies to maximize your time and revenue', 'structured roadmap to success'.

evidence.quote: we provide aspiring entrepreneurs with the mentorship, tools, and exclusive discounts they need to launch and scale a profitable vending machine business with minimal time investment.

persona_rationale: This reads like a LinkedIn ad my dad would screenshot, not something made for me. 'Mentorship, tools, and exclusive discounts to launch and scale' is the kind of stacked business-speak my eyes slide right off. Brands I follow talk like a person.

suggested_fix.current: 'launch and scale a profitable vending machine business with minimal time investment'
suggested_fix.proposed: Plain, human line like 'Start a vending business without the guesswork — we show you exactly how, step by step.'

finding: The primary nav CTA is labelled 'STEP INSIDE' — it's vague and doesn't say it opens an application form.
persona_rationale: 'STEP INSIDE' sounds like a vibe, not a button — I have no idea it's the apply form until I tap it. Buttons I'm used to say exactly what happens ('Sign up', 'Get started'). The bright orange button screaming 'STEP INSIDE' is mysterious in a way that makes me not tap it.

suggested_fix.proposed: 'Apply now' or 'Get started' to match the page it opens

finding: There's no way to reach Contact from the top menu — it only exists as a link in the footer, so you have to scroll all the way to the bottom of the page to find it.

evidence.quote: navigation "Primary": link "About" / link "Resources" / link "Case Studies" / link "News" (no Contact)
Contact is not in the header navigation; the only paths are the footer link and knowing the URL.

persona_rationale: I never scroll to the bottom of anything — the footer is dead space to me. If I wanted to ask a question I'd look at the top, not find Contact, and assume there's no way to message them. Social apps put DM/message right up top.

suggested_fix.current: Contact link only in the footer
suggested_fix.proposed: Add 'Contact' to the primary header nav (or a persistent message/chat affordance)

finding: Mobile homepage has horizontal scrolling — the page can be dragged sideways, which feels glitchy on a phone.
persona_rationale: Everything I use is locked to vertical scroll. When a page slides sideways under my thumb it feels broken and cheap — like a website that wasn't actually made for phones, which is the only thing I use.

suggested_fix.current: Horizontal scroll present at 375px width on the homepage
suggested_fix.proposed: Constrain content to 100% viewport width / fix the overflowing element so the page only scrolls vertically on mobile

finding: Noticeable content jump on load — layout shift (CLS) of 0.263 on the homepage and most public pages.
persona_rationale: When stuff jumps around as a page loads I end up tapping the wrong thing — I'm used to feeds that lock in place instantly. A page that shoves itself around for a second feels old and laggy.

suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve space for the hero image and partner-logo strip (set width/height) so nothing shifts after paint; target CLS < 0.1

finding: Empty/invalid form submit only shows the browser's default 'Please fill out this field.' tooltip — no styled inline errors next to each field.

evidence.quote: default browser bubble 'Please fill out this field.' pointing at Phone
empty: no visible validation errors after empty submit

persona_rationale: The little grey browser bubble looks like a system pop-up, not part of the app — it's the boomer-software default. Forms I use highlight the field in red right where the problem is. This feels unfinished.

suggested_fix.current: Relies on native browser validation tooltips only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add styled inline field-level errors (red outline + message under the field) for required fields

finding: The About page is several dense paragraphs of life story ('How I Escaped The Rat Race', 'Mike's Unique Approach') with no short summary or skimmable highlights.

evidence.quote: At the time, I was working a 60-hour per week job that paid me only $1,200/month. I used my emergency fund to invest 20% down on a $70,000 fixer upper...

persona_rationale: That's a wall of text. I don't read paragraphs — I skim for the gist in two seconds like a caption. There's no TL;DR, no bullet highlights, so I'd scroll straight past and never learn who Mike is.

suggested_fix.current: Multi-paragraph founder essay with no summary
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 3-bullet 'the short version' at the top (e.g. 'Quit a 60-hr job', 'Built passive income with vending', '12+ machines now') above the full story
